papers.title: MANSION: Multi-floor lANguage-to-3D Scene generatIOn for loNg-horizon tasks
papers.abstract: Real-world robotic tasks are long-horizon and often span multiple floors, demanding rich spatial reasoning. However, existing embodied benchmarks are largely confined to single-floor in-house environments, failing to reflect the complexity of real-world tasks. We introduce MANSION, the first language-driven framework for generating building-scale, multi-floor 3D environments. Being aware of vertical structural constraints, MANSION generates realistic, navigable whole-building structures with diverse, human-friendly scenes, enabling the development and evaluation of cross-floor long-horizon tasks. Building on this framework, we release MansionWorld, a dataset of over 1,000 diverse buildings ranging from hospitals to offices, alongside a Task-Semantic Scene Editing Agent that customizes these environments using open-vocabulary commands to meet specific user needs. Benchmarking reveals that state-of-the-art agents degrade sharply in our settings, establishing MANSION as a critical testbed for the next generation of spatial reasoning and planning.

papers.title: Learning to Assist: Physics-Grounded Human-Human Control via Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Humanoid robotics has strong potential to transform daily service and caregiving applications. Although recent advances in general motion tracking within physics engines (GMT) have enabled virtual characters and humanoid robots to reproduce a broad range of human motions, these behaviors are primarily limited to contact-less social interactions or isolated movements. Assistive scenarios, by contrast, require continuous awareness of a human partner and rapid adaptation to their evolving posture and dynamics. In this paper, we formulate the imitation of closely interacting, force-exchanging human-human motion sequences as a multi-agent reinforcement learning problem. We jointly train partner-aware policies for both the supporter (assistant) agent and the recipient agent in a physics simulator to track assistive motion references. To make this problem tractable, we introduce a partner policies initialization scheme that transfers priors from single-human motion-tracking controllers, greatly improving exploration. We further propose dynamic reference retargeting and contact-promoting reward, which adapt the assistant's reference motion to the recipient's real-time pose and encourage physically meaningful support. We show that AssistMimic is the first method capable of successfully tracking assistive interaction motions on established benchmarks, demonstrating the benefits of a multi-agent RL formulation for physically grounded and socially aware humanoid control.

papers.title: Multi-Robot Multitask Gaussian Process Estimation and Coverage
papers.abstract: Coverage control is essential for the optimal deployment of agents to monitor or cover areas with sensory demands. While traditional coverage involves single-task robots, increasing autonomy now enables multitask operations. This paper introduces a novel multitask coverage problem and addresses it for both the cases of known and unknown sensory demands. For known demands, we design a federated multitask coverage algorithm and establish its convergence properties. For unknown demands, we employ a multitask Gaussian Process (GP) framework to learn sensory demand functions and integrate it with the multitask coverage algorithm to develop an adaptive algorithm. We introduce a novel notion of multitask coverage regret that compares the performance of the adaptive algorithm against an oracle with prior knowledge of the demand functions. We establish that our algorithm achieves sublinear cumulative regret, and numerically illustrate its performance.

papers.title: RC-NF: Robot-Conditioned Normalizing Flow for Real-Time Anomaly Detection in Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract: Recent advances in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have enabled robots to execute increasingly complex tasks. However, VLA models trained through imitation learning struggle to operate reliably in dynamic environments and often fail under Out-of-Distribution (OOD) conditions. To address this issue, we propose Robot-Conditioned Normalizing Flow (RC-NF), a real-time monitoring model for robotic anomaly detection and intervention that ensures the robot's state and the object's motion trajectory align with the task. RC-NF decouples the processing of task-aware robot and object states within the normalizing flow. It requires only positive samples for unsupervised training and calculates accurate robotic anomaly scores during inference through the probability density function. We further present LIBERO-Anomaly-10, a benchmark comprising three categories of robotic anomalies for simulation evaluation. RC-NF achieves state-of-the-art performance across all anomaly types compared to previous methods in monitoring robotic tasks. Real-world experiments demonstrate that RC-NF operates as a plug-and-play module for VLA models (e.g., pi0), providing a real-time OOD signal that enables state-level rollback or task-level replanning when necessary, with a response latency under 100 ms. These results demonstrate that RC-NF noticeably enhances the robustness and adaptability of VLA-based robotic systems in dynamic environments.

papers.title: A Survey of Reasoning in Autonomous Driving Systems: Open Challenges and Emerging Paradigms
papers.abstract: The development of high-level autonomous driving (AD) is shifting from perception-centric limitations to a more fundamental bottleneck, namely, a deficit in robust and generalizable reasoning. Although current AD systems manage structured environments, they consistently falter in long-tail scenarios and complex social interactions that require human-like judgment. Meanwhile, the advent of large language and multimodal models (LLMs and MLLMs) presents a transformative opportunity to integrate a powerful cognitive engine into AD systems, moving beyond pattern matching toward genuine comprehension. However, a systematic framework to guide this integration is critically lacking. To bridge this gap, we provide a comprehensive review of this emerging field and argue that reasoning should be elevated from a modular component to the system's cognitive core. Specifically, we first propose a novel Cognitive Hierarchy to decompose the monolithic driving task according to its cognitive and interactive complexity. Building on this, we further derive and systematize seven core reasoning challenges, such as the responsiveness-reasoning trade-off and social-game reasoning. Furthermore, we conduct a dual-perspective review of the state-of-the-art, analyzing both system-centric approaches to architecting intelligent agents and evaluation-centric practices for their validation. Our analysis reveals a clear trend toward holistic and interpretable "glass-box" agents. In conclusion, we identify a fundamental and unresolved tension between the high-latency, deliberative nature of LLM-based reasoning and the millisecond-scale, safety-critical demands of vehicle control. For future work, a primary objective is to bridge the symbolic-to-physical gap by developing verifiable neuro-symbolic architectures, robust reasoning under uncertainty, and scalable models for implicit social negotiation.

executive_summary: Strong focus on dexterous and whole-body manipulation: new frameworks span bimanual piano playing, humanoid loco-manipulation, and concurrent prehensile/nonprehensile tasks.
Motion planning advances include ADMM-based continuous trajectory optimization, tensor-compressed configuration-space exploration, and a robust reduced-space trajectory optimizer.
Humanoid and hand hardware/teleop pipelines (SPARK, HumDex, CRAFT, vision-based hand shadowing) lower the barrier for collecting high-quality whole-body and hand manipulation data.
Foundation and agentic VLA models (Ψ₀, HumDex’s IL, SaPaVe, RoboClaw, SELF-VLA, SVLL) target long-horizon, active, and contact-rich manipulation with improved data efficiency and robustness.
Reliability and safety around learned policies are addressed via deployment-time mechanisms, anomaly detection, load distribution theory, and data-grounded generalization analysis.
Autonomous data engines (RADAR, RoboClaw) and evaluation tools (RADAR analysis, ActiveManip-Bench, LIBERO-Anomaly-10) emphasize scalable, closed-loop data generation and benchmarking.

institution_matches.title: HandelBot: Real-World Piano Playing via Fast Adaptation of Dexterous Robot Policies
institution_matches.overview: Introduces a two-stage adaptation pipeline that refines a simulation-trained dexterous bimanual hand policy for millimeter-precision piano playing. Structured spatial refinement and residual RL enable successful execution of five songs with only ~30 minutes of real data, achieving 1.8× better hardware performance than direct sim-to-real transfer.

institution_matches.title: SPARK: Skeleton-Parameter Aligned Retargeting on Humanoid Robots with Kinodynamic Trajectory Optimization
institution_matches.overview: Proposes a URDF-based skeleton alignment scheme plus staged kinodynamic trajectory optimization to retarget human motion onto diverse humanoids. The method reduces IK error and tuning effort while producing dynamically feasible motion and torque profiles suitable as references for learning-based humanoid controllers.

institution_matches.title: ADMM-based Continuous Trajectory Optimization in Graphs of Convex Sets
institution_matches.overview: Develops an ADMM-based solver that represents trajectories as polynomials and couples them with a spatio-temporal allocation graph derived from a mixed-integer formulation. By jointly optimizing discrete spatial choices and continuous time, it finds higher-quality trajectories in non-convex environments and converges reliably from naive initializations.

institution_matches.title: Grounding Robot Generalization in Training Data via Retrieval-Augmented VLMs
institution_matches.overview: Introduces RADAR, a retrieval-augmented VLM pipeline for characterizing how evaluation tasks relate to a policy’s training distribution. By retrieving similar training episodes and using VLM analysis, it classifies the type of generalization required and provides interpretable diagnostics for robot manipulation benchmarks.

institution_matches.title: Vision-Based Hand Shadowing for Robotic Manipulation via Inverse Kinematics
institution_matches.overview: Presents a low-cost egocentric RGB-D teleoperation pipeline that tracks human hands with MediaPipe, lifts to 3D, and solves IK in PyBullet to control a 6-DOF manipulator and gripper. Achieves 90% success on structured pick-and-place and compares against several VLA policies, while revealing limitations under occlusion in clutter.

institution_matches.title: Real-time Rendering-based Surgical Instrument Tracking via Evolutionary Optimization
institution_matches.overview: Combines CMA-ES with batch rendering to jointly estimate pose and joint angles of articulated surgical tools from images. The evolutionary rendering-in-the-loop approach improves robustness and runtime over prior vision-based trackers, supporting both mono- and bi-manual tracking in surgical settings.

institution_matches.title: ResWM: Residual-Action World Model for Visual RL
institution_matches.overview: Introduces a Dreamer-style world model that plans over residual (incremental) actions instead of absolute commands, coupled with an observation-difference encoder. This residual formulation stabilizes optimization, improves sample efficiency and returns on DeepMind Control, and yields smoother, more energy-efficient control trajectories for robotics.

topic_matches.title: $\Psi_0$: An Open Foundation Model Towards Universal Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: Proposes Ψ₀, a staged foundation model that first pre-trains a VLM backbone on egocentric human manipulation videos, then post-trains a flow-based action head on humanoid data for precise joint control. With ~800 hours of human video and 30 hours of robot data, it surpasses baselines trained on >10× data by over 40% on real-world humanoid loco-manipulation tasks.

topic_matches.title: HumDex:Humanoid Dexterous Manipulation Made Easy
topic_matches.overview: Introduces HumDex, a portable IMU-based teleoperation system for humanoid whole-body dexterous manipulation, including a learning-based hand retargeting module. A two-stage imitation learning pipeline pre-trains on human motion then fine-tunes on robot data, improving generalization in complex whole-body manipulation with low data collection cost.

topic_matches.title: Coupling Tensor Trains with Graph of Convex Sets: Effective Compression, Exploration, and Planning in the C-Space
topic_matches.institution: Northeastern University; Technical University of Munich
topic_matches.overview: TANGO combines Tensor Train decomposition to compactly approximate feasible configuration space with a GCS-like graph for trajectory optimization. This hybrid representation enables efficient discovery of task-relevant regions and generation of high-quality, geometry-aware motion plans in high-dimensional systems.

topic_matches.title: Concurrent Prehensile and Nonprehensile Manipulation: A Practical Approach to Multi-Stage Dexterous Tasks
topic_matches.overview: Presents DexMulti, which decomposes long-horizon concurrent dexterous tasks into object-centric skills and uses a retrieve-align-execute paradigm guided by geometry and pose uncertainty. On three multi-stage tasks with Allegro and LEAP hands, it attains 66% average success with only 3–4 demos per object, outperforming diffusion-policy baselines by 2–3×.

topic_matches.title: DRAFTO: Decoupled Reduced-space and Adaptive Feasibility-repair Trajectory Optimization for Robotic Manipulators
topic_matches.overview: Introduces DRAFTO, which parameterizes trajectories via basis functions and applies reduced-space Gauss-Newton optimization combined with QP-based initialization and feasibility repair. A non-monotone acceptance policy with hinge-squared penalties yields globally reliable, efficient motion plans that outperform CHOMP, TrajOpt, GPMP2, FACTO, and standard sampling planners across benchmarks.

topic_matches.title: SaPaVe: Towards Active Perception and Manipulation in Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotics
topic_matches.overview: SaPaVe decouples camera and manipulation actions in a VLA framework, first learning semantic camera control from a 200k-sample ActiveViewPose dataset, then jointly training for active manipulation. With a 3D geometry-aware module and the new ActiveManip-Bench, it substantially improves robustness to viewpoint changes, beating GR00T N1 and π₀ in simulation and real-world tasks.

topic_matches.title: CRAFT: A Tendon-Driven Hand with Hybrid Hard-Soft Compliance
topic_matches.overview: Describes the CRAFT anthropomorphic hand, which places soft, rolling-contact joints between rigid links to localize compliance where impacts occur while maintaining structural strength. A 15-motor tendon drive yields lightweight fingers capable of all 33 Feix grasps, improved handling of fragile/low-friction objects, and teleoperation-ready, sub-$600 open-source hardware.

topic_matches.title: RADAR: Closed-Loop Robotic Data Generation via Semantic Planning and Autonomous Causal Environment Reset
topic_matches.overview: Proposes a fully autonomous four-module engine that uses a VLM for task generation and evaluation, a GNN policy for execution, and an FSM for causal environment resetting. Guided by a few 3D human demos, RADAR achieves up to 90% success on complex simulated tasks and robust real-world contact-rich skills without human resets, enabling scalable closed-loop data collection.

topic_matches.title: Learn Structure, Adapt on the Fly: Multi-Scale Residual Learning and Online Adaptation for Aerial Manipulators
topic_matches.overview: Introduces a Factorized Dynamics Transformer that treats physical variables as tokens to capture cross-variable coupling and multi-scale temporal dynamics in aerial manipulators. A lightweight Latent Residual Adapter performs fast online adaptation via RLS, yielding more accurate residual modeling and better closed-loop tracking under unseen payloads and configurations.

topic_matches.title: RoboClaw: An Agentic Framework for Scalable Long-Horizon Robotic Tasks
topic_matches.overview: RoboClaw unifies data collection, learning, and execution under a single VLM-driven agent that uses Entangled Action Pairs to couple forward skills with recovery behaviors for self-resetting. This agentic design supports continuous on-policy data acquisition and robust composition of primitives, boosting long-horizon manipulation success by 25% and halving human time compared to conventional pipelines.
